rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Demurrer And Demurrers To Complaint
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for September 16, 2022 line 1. RESPONDENT WILMINGTON SAVINGS FUND SOCIETY , FSB D/B/A Notice Of Demurrer And Demurrers To Complaint is SUSTAINED with leave to amend to address in good faith all issues raised by demurrer and/or allege any viable cause of action. =(501/CFH) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849; or Phone Dial in: (551) 285-1373; Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849). Parties who intend to appear at the hearing must give notice to opposing parties and the court promptly, but no later than 4:00 p.m. the court day before the hearing unless the tentative ruling has specified that a hearing is required. Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not so notified, and the opposing party does not appear.

rulings.Case Title: FREDERICK HUTCHINS MOLL VS. JUDITH M BRASS ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Defendant Judith M. Brass' Notice Of Motion And Motion To Reopen Discovery Pursuant To Ccp 2024.050 - Discovery
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for September 16, 2022 line 2. DEFENDANT JUDITH BRASS Defendant Judith M. Brass' Notice Of Motion And Motion To Reopen Discovery Pursuant To Ccp 2024.050 - Discovery is OFF CALENDAR for department 501. This action was assigned to a trial department on August 17, 2022. Additionally, pre-trial discovery motions in general civil cases must be first set for 9:00 a.m. in department 301. =(501/CFH) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849; or Phone Dial in: (551) 285-1373; Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849). rulings.Case Title: WILLIAM BYERLY VS. 2100 GREEN STREET, INC. ET AL
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for September 16, 2022 line 3. PLAINTIFF WILLIAM BYERLEY MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION is DENIED. Triable issue of fact exists whether the roof(s) at issues are "occupied roof(s)" within the meaning of the Building Code. Additionally, motion for summary adjudication is proper only if it disposes of an entire cause of action and such motion is framed by the pleadings. The fifth cause of action in the fifth amended complaint seeks money damages and punitive damages (paragraphs 91 and 92), the issue of damages is not disposed of by this motion. =(501/CFH) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849; or Phone Dial in: (551) 285-1373; Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849). Parties who intend to appear at the hearing must give notice to opposing parties and the court promptly, but no later than 4:00 p.m. the court day before the hearing unless the tentative ruling has specified that a hearing is required. Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not so notified, and the opposing party does not appear.

rulings.Case Title: LEELANAU HOLDINGS LLC, A DELAWARE LIMITED VS. MASON STREET AFFORDABLE HOUSING, L.P., A ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: DEFENDANT'S MASON STREET AFFORDABLE HOUSING L.P'S MOTION FOR APPOINTMENT OF RECEIVER AND PRELIMINARY INJUNCTION
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for September 16, 2022 line 6. PLAINTIFF LEELANAU HOLDINGS LLC'S MOTION FOR APPOINTMENT OF RECEIVER AND PRELIMINARY INJUNCTION is continued to January 6, 2023. No later than September 16, 2022 by 5:00 p.m. defendant to pay $139,945.61 to reinstate the loan. Compliance hearing shall be set for September 20, 2022. No appearance for the hearing is required if a declaration of compliance is filed no later than noon on September 19, 2022. Thereafter Defendant shall remit monthly payment due under the loan on the first business day of each month in October, November, December 2022 and January 2023. Complaint hearings shall be set for the 3rd business day of each month. No appearance is required if a declaration of compliance is filed by noon on the second business day of each month. If Defendant fails to timely make any of the payments listed above the Court will grant the motion to appoint a receiver. Supplemental briefs for January 6, 2023 (not to exceed 5 pages) may be filed no later than January 4, 2023. =(501/CFH) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849; or Phone Dial in: (551) 285-1373; Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849). Parties who intend to appear at the hearing must give notice to opposing parties and the court promptly, but no later than 4:00 p.m. the court day before the hearing unless the tentative ruling has specified that a hearing is required. Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. rulings.Case Title: TRINITY MANAGEMENT SERVICES, INC., A CALIFORNIA VS. LUDMILA KRYUCHKOVA ET AL
rulings.Rulings: DEFENDANT LUDMILA KRYUCHKOVA MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for September 16, 2022 line 7. In light of the inconsistencies between allegations in the operative complaint and exhibits thereto, as well as evidence submitted in support of the Opposition to the Motion for Summary Judgment, specifically declaration of Jacki Lopez paragraph 3, the Court will treat this motion as a motion for judgment on the pleadings. Motion for Judgment on the Pleadings is GRANTED with leave to amend for Plaintiff to reconcile in good faith allegations in the Complaint (paragraph 7, listing original monthly rent as $3,245), Lease listing the rent for the premises as defined in paragraph 2 as $2,950 and no parking per paragraph 23, with the Lopez Declaration listing total original rent as $3,200 (compare to complaint paragraph 7 at $3,245) including $250 for parking (compare to Lease paragraphs 2 and 53). Trial date is vacated. =(501/CFH) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849; or Phone Dial in: (551) 285-1373; Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849). Parties who intend to appear at the hearing must give notice to opposing parties and the court promptly, but no later than 4:00 p.m. the court day before the hearing unless the tentative ruling has specified that a hearing is required. Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not so notified, and the opposing party does not appear.
